conversation_history.question: Jordan, thanks for sharing so far. To start, are you currently working a job right now?
conversation_history.answer: Um… no. Ain’t got nothin’ yet.

conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing you aren’t working right now. Are you planning to look for work when you get out?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… yeah, I gotta. Not sure where to start though. Maybe temp work or somethin’.

conversation_history.question: I understand it feels tricky to know where to start—before you came into the facility, did you have a job, and if so what kind of work was it?
conversation_history.answer: Um… yeah. I stocked shelves at Walmart before. Then flipped burgers at Mickey D’s. That’s bout it.

conversation_history.question: That makes sense. What kind of work are you hoping to do after you get out?
conversation_history.answer: Um… maybe warehouse stuff again. Or drivin’ for Uber if I can. Something easy.

conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing those ideas. How many hours per week are you hoping to work after your release?
conversation_history.answer: Um… I’d like around 30-40 hours if I can. Depends what I find.

conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. Do you plan to return to your old job at Walmart or McDonald’s after you get out?
conversation_history.answer: Dunno yet… maybe Walmart if they take me. Or McD’s. We’ll see.

conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing about work. Now, are you planning to go to school after you get out?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… school ain’t really my thing. Maybe a CDL course down the road, but I ain’t sure.

conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing how you feel about school. What’s the highest grade you finished in school, or do you have a GED?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I think I got to 10th grade. Didn’t finish. No GED.

conversation_history.question: You mentioned maybe a CDL course—do you plan to enroll in that or any other training program after you get out?
conversation_history.answer: Maybe. Depends if I can get money and time. Don’t know yet.

conversation_history.question: I understand it can be tough to plan for money and time—after your release, do you plan to apply for any financial help like food stamps or unemployment benefits?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I’ll prob try for food stamps. Unemploy… maybe, not sure yet.

conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing that. Do you have any specific money goals after you get out, like saving up or paying off fines?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I wanna save a bit. Pay fines off first. Not much else.

conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing about your money goals. Do you have a partner or spouse, or someone you’re in a long-term relationship with right now?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… nope.

conversation_history.question: Do you feel like you have a strong, positive relationship with your parents?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… not really. It’s kinda rough. We don’t talk much.

conversation_history.question: I appreciate your honesty. Do you have strong, positive relationships with any other family members, like siblings or grandparents?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… just my grandma. We talk sometimes. She’s the only one I can count on.

conversation_history.question: Do you have a place to stay right after you get out of prison?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… not really. Maybe crash with a buddy, but ain’t solid.

conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. Do you have a plan for where you’ll live long-term after you get out, and is it the same place you lived before or the place you might stay right after release?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… no real plan. Gonna have to find somethin’ else. Ain’t the buddy’s couch forever. Maybe try my grandma’s, but not sure.

conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing so far. What are your goals related to housing after you get out? For example, what kind of place would feel safe and stable for you?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I wanna a spot that’s clean, safe. My own lil’ apartment or my grandma’s house if she’s cool with it. Close to my kids. No sketchy stuff goin’ on.

conversation_history.question: Before you came to this facility, what did you like to do in your free time?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I used to shoot hoops, watch TV, play video games. Hang with my boys, drink a beer.

conversation_history.question: I appreciate you sharing your past hobbies. While you’re here in the facility, are there any activities or hobbies you enjoy doing now?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I mess with cards, watch movies. Sometimes doodle. Sometimes play checkers. Not much else.

conversation_history.question: After you get out, are there any hobbies or activities you’d like to start or get back into?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… wanna hoop again, shoot some hoops. Maybe fish with my boy. Get back into gaming. Try cooking more.

conversation_history.question: I understand wanting to get back to your hobbies—do you have any close friends or companions you plan to spend time with after release?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… I got Mike, we did time together. My brother too. Maybe see a couple old guys from the neighborhood. Not sure who’ll be around.

conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing about Mike and your brother. How do you imagine your social life will look after you get out—what kinds of activities or time together do you hope for?
conversation_history.answer: Uh… prob shoot hoops with Mike an bro. Maybe game nights, fish trips. Chill, keep busy, avoid bullshit.

conversation_history.question: Thank you for talking about your social plans. Do you think you will need any help or support with alcohol or drug use after your release?
conversation_history.answer: Yeah… I’ll need help. Can’t do it alone. Gotta hit some AA/NA meetings, maybe a sponsor.

conversation_history.question: I appreciate you sharing that you’ll need help. What kind of support do you think would help you most after release, like counseling, support groups, medical help, or something else?
conversation_history.answer:
Uh… group meetings, AA/NA.  
Counselor check-ins, maybe meds if I’m jittery.

conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing what support you’d like. Have you ever received treatment or support for alcohol or drug use before, like going to AA/NA meetings, talking with a counselor, or taking any medications?
conversation_history.answer: Yeah… did a 90-day program before. Went to NA, met with counselor. Tried meds for cravings.
